title.en: DAIMO: an ontology for governed AI model assets in dataspaces

description.en: DAIMO is an integration profile that connects DCAT-AP, MLDCAT-AP, ODRL, PROV-O, and the Dataspace Protocol (DSP, as operationalised by Eclipse EDC) to support publication, discovery, invocation, execution traceability, and contextualised evaluation of AI model assets exchanged in dataspaces. The edc: namespace is used only for EDC-specific extensions (e.g., ParticipantContext) not yet standardised in DSP.

comments.en: See the companion documents ONTOLOGY-REFERENCE.md (class-by-class reference), VALIDATION-MATRIX.md (traceability), and daimo-ontology-design.md (design rationale) in the source repository.

title.value: DAIMO: an ontology for governed AI model assets in dataspaces

definition.value: An ODRL agreement, produced by a Dataspace-Protocol contract negotiation, that binds specific ODRL permissions to concrete MLDCAT-AP run invocations it authorises, with an expiry timestamp and a grantee agent.

example.value: After a successful DSP contract negotiation, the resulting agreement is represented as an ExecutionAuthorization granting odrl:use to the consumer (daimo:grantedTo), covering one or more runs via daimo:authorizesRun, expiring at a specified daimo:expiresAt timestamp.

comment.en: An ODRL agreement (produced by a DSP contract negotiation) that binds at least one odrl:permission to concrete it6:Run invocations it authorises. Specialises odrl:Agreement; MUST carry at least one odrl:permission per the ODRL policy model.

definition.value: Role of an agent responsible for operating the dataspace runtime, hosting model deployments, and enforcing policy at runtime.

example.value: A dataspace platform provider hosting one or more ModelDeployments, signing AuditEvidence records, and running the EDC connector acts as a PlatformOperator in each participant context it administers.

comment.en: Role of an agent responsible for operating the dataspace runtime, hosting model deployments, and enforcing policy.

definition.value: A compliance-oriented evidence record attached to a governed activity, carrying a structured SPDX checksum (algorithm + digest), a signer, and a timestamp.

example.value: For each run of a deployed model, the PlatformOperator produces an AuditEvidence record that references the run (daimo:evidenceOf), carries a structured spdx:Checksum (algorithm + digest) over the run's outputs (daimo:integrityHash), identifies the signer (daimo:signedBy), and timestamps the sealing (daimo:recordedAt).

comment.en: A compliance-oriented evidence record attached to a governed event, carrying integrity hash, signer, and timestamp.

definition.value: A dataspace-scoped role played by a participant with respect to AI assets (anti-rigid; an agent gains and loses roles over time).

example.value: An agent acting as a Model Provider within a given EDC participant context has a ParticipantRole instance of type ModelProvider connected via daimo:hasRole and scoped by daimo:inParticipantContext.

comment.en: A dataspace-scoped role taken by a participant with respect to AI assets. Anti-rigid: an agent can gain and lose roles over time. Subclasses are intentionally NOT mutually disjoint — one agent can hold several roles simultaneously (e.g., a research group may be both Evaluator and Model Provider).

definition.value: Role of an agent that audits execution evidence, enforces compliance obligations, and validates governance across the dataspace.

example.value: An external compliance body that reads CrossParticipantProvenanceRecord bundles and AuditEvidence records to verify lawful execution holds a GovernanceActor role — for example, a regulator or a conformance-labelling body.

comment.en: Role of an agent that audits execution evidence, enforces compliance obligations, and validates governance across the dataspace.

definition.value: A running or hosted instance of a machine-learning model exposed through one or more DCAT data services over a named compute infrastructure, under a per-service I/O contract.

example.value: A PlatformOperator that stands up a live inference endpoint for a given model creates a ModelDeployment that (i) deploys the model, (ii) is exposed as one or more dcat:DataServices (e.g., REST + gRPC), (iii) runs on a specific it6:ComputerInfrastructure, and (iv) declares an IOContract per exposed service.

comment.en: A running or hosted instance of an MLDCAT-AP MachineLearningModel exposed through a dcat:DataService under an operational environment.

definition.value: A PROV bundle that aggregates activities and entities spanning at least two EDC participant contexts, forming an audit-ready cross-participant provenance narrative.

example.value: When a derived artefact is produced by a consumer invoking a provider's model hosted by a third-party operator, a CrossParticipantProvenanceRecord aggregates the relevant activities across the provider, consumer, and operator participant contexts into one bundle for audit.

comment.en: A reusable semantic bundle that aggregates prov:Activity and prov:Entity across participant contexts to tell one audit-ready story.

definition.value: A governed, catalog-describable output produced by a run in a dataspace context, carrying its own provenance pointer and authorisation pointer.

example.value: When a consumer invokes a deployed model, the run's output (a prediction dataset, a classification result, a generated artefact) is typed as a DerivedArtifact that points back to the producing run (daimo:derivedFromRun) and to the authorisation under which it was produced (daimo:underAuthorization).

comment.en: A governed, catalog-describable output produced by an it6:Run in a dataspace context, bearing its own provenance and policy.

definition.value: Role of an agent that discovers, negotiates access to, and invokes AI models offered by a Model Provider.

example.value: Any agent that receives an ExecutionAuthorization (via daimo:grantedTo) and uses it to invoke runs of a deployed model holds a ModelConsumer role within the corresponding participant context.

comment.en: Role of an agent that discovers, negotiates, and invokes AI models offered by a Model Provider.

definition.value: Role of an agent that publishes AI models as governed offerings in a dataspace catalog.

example.value: Any organisation that declares one or more AIAssetOfferings under its own authorship and policy holds a ModelProvider role in the dataspace contexts where those offerings are listed.

comment.en: Role of an agent that publishes one or more AI models as governed offerings in a dataspace catalog.

definition.value: A catalog-record entry registering an AI model in a dataspace catalog as a governed offer, bundling the model, the issuing agent, and an ODRL offer policy.

example.value: A research organisation publishing one of its machine-learning models into a dataspace catalog declares an AIAssetOffering that references the model (via offersModel), the organisation itself (via offeredBy), and an ODRL offer policy (via hasOfferPolicy) permitting use and distribution under a specific licence.

comment.en: A catalog-record entry that registers an AI model in a dataspace catalog, bundling the model reference, the providing agent, and an ODRL offer policy. Specialises dcat:CatalogRecord; carries an odrl:Offer via daimo:hasOfferPolicy. In DSP terms this is the catalog-time artefact that becomes a dspace:ContractOffer during negotiation.

definition.value: A reified grouping of the conditions under which model evaluations are comparable: task, dataset, dataset version, evaluation protocol, and random seed.

example.value: Two candidate models evaluated on the same SharedEvaluationContext (identical it6:Task, dcat:Dataset, daimo:datasetVersion, daimo:protocol, and daimo:randomSeed) yield metric values that are directly comparable; changing any of the five facets breaks comparability.

comment.en: A reified grouping of the conditions under which model results are comparable: task, dataset, dataset version, protocol, and random seed. Does not claim to cover every facet that affects comparability (metric, split strategy, hyperparameters); it is the minimum grouping required by CQ-V1..CQ-V3.

definition.value: Role of an agent that evaluates or compares AI models under a shared evaluation context.

example.value: Any agent that produces it6:Evaluation instances linked to a shared SharedEvaluationContext (via daimo:usesEvaluationContext) holds an Evaluator role — for example, a benchmarking team comparing candidate models on the same dataset version and seed.

comment.en: Role of an agent that evaluates or compares AI models under a shared evaluation context.

definition.value: The minimum machine-actionable invocation contract for a deployment endpoint: input media type, output media type, authentication method, and optional input/output schemas.

example.value: A REST inference endpoint might declare an IOContract with inputFormat "application/json", outputFormat "application/geo+json", and authMethod "oauth2-bearer"; a parallel gRPC endpoint on the same deployment can declare a second IOContract with inputFormat "application/x-protobuf" and authMethod "mtls".

comment.en: The minimum machine-actionable invocation contract for a deployment: input format, output format, authentication method, and optional input/output schemas.
Authentication-method token (e.g. "oauth2-bearer", "api-key", "mtls").
Links an offering to the ODRL offer policy that governs the offered model. Functional: each offering carries exactly one canonical offer policy.

Links an authorization to a run it permits. Non-functional: a single authorization can cover multiple runs (e.g., subscription-style access). Asymmetric: an authorization is distinct from any run it authorises. Deliberately NOT declared a subproperty of prov:used: an odrl:Agreement is a policy artefact (an Entity), not a prov:Activity; the prov:used domain would incorrectly force ExecutionAuthorization ⊑ prov:Activity.

Links an offering to the agent that authored the offered AI model (i.e., the model provider). Functional: each offering has exactly one offering agent. Deliberately NOT declared rdfs:subPropertyOf dct:publisher: on a dcat:CatalogRecord, dct:publisher denotes the catalog maintainer (the platform registering the record), not the author of the asset being registered. Conflating them would entail that the catalog-time publisher is the model creator — semantically wrong in a real dataspace where e.g. INESData publishes records authored by UPM.
The it6:Task the evaluation context is defined over. Functional.
Functional: one signer per evidence record.
Links a cross-participant provenance bundle to each prov:Activity that forms part of the audit-ready narrative.

The dcat:Dataset the evaluation context is defined over. Functional. Not declared a subproperty of it6:trainedOn because an evaluation-context dataset is an evaluation dataset, not necessarily a training dataset.
Optional link to a resource describing the output payload schema.
Links an agent to a dataspace role. Non-functional: agents may hold multiple roles.
Random seed fixing stochastic components of the protocol, required for reproducibility.
IANA media type of the produced output payload (e.g. "application/geo+json").

Literal version identifier of the evaluation dataset used by the context. Deliberately not aligned to dct:hasVersion because dct:hasVersion relates a resource to another version resource, whereas DAIMO records the dataset-version token as a literal for reproducible benchmarking.
Links an it6:Evaluation to the shared evaluation context under which its results are comparable to other evaluations. Functional.

Links a deployment to its invocation contract(s). Non-functional: a deployment exposing multiple services (see daimo:exposedAs) may carry a distinct IOContract per protocol (e.g., JSON+OAuth2 for REST and protobuf+mTLS for gRPC).
Timestamp at which the audit evidence was sealed.
Inverse of daimo:derivedFromRun: from a run, retrieve every derived artifact it produced. Non-functional (a run may produce multiple governed outputs).

Links an authorization to the grantee agent. Functional. Specialises odrl:assignee (the ODRL party receiving an agreement's terms). Deliberately NOT aligned to prov:qualifiedAssociation: that property points to a reified prov:Association object, not to the agent itself; using it here would incorrectly type every grantee Agent as a prov:Association.

Links audit evidence to the activity it attests. Functional. Asymmetric: audit evidence is distinct from the activity it records. Deliberately NOT declared a subproperty of prov:hadActivity: that property is used on reified influence/generation/usage objects, not on entities — an AuditEvidence is a prov:Entity that is ABOUT an activity, which PROV-O does not provide a direct property for.

Links audit evidence to an spdx:Checksum that bundles the algorithm (spdx:algorithm) and the digest value (spdx:checksumValue). Functional: exactly one canonical checksum per evidence record. A plain-string digest without its algorithm cannot be verified, so a structured spdx:Checksum is required rather than an xsd:string.
Scopes a role assignment to a specific EDC participant context. Functional: a role instance is held within exactly one context.
Links a deployment to the model it serves. Functional: one deployment serves one model. Asymmetric: a deployment and its model are distinct individuals.
Optional link to a resource describing the input payload schema (JSON Schema, SHACL shape, etc.).

Links a deployment to the DCAT data service(s) that expose it. Non-functional: one deployment may expose multiple endpoints (e.g., a REST service plus a gRPC service, or regional load-balanced endpoints). Each service's I/O contract can differ (see daimo:hasIOContract, also non-functional).

Inverse of daimo:offersModel: from a model, retrieve every catalog offering that registers it. Non-functional (the same model may be offered by different providers in different catalogs). Declared rdfs:subPropertyOf foaf:isPrimaryTopicOf for symmetry with daimo:offersModel ⊑ foaf:primaryTopic.
Inverse of daimo:deploysModel: from a model, retrieve every deployment that serves it. Non-functional (a model can have multiple deployments across contexts).
Links a cross-participant provenance bundle to each participant context whose activities or entities it records. A record must span at least two contexts (enforced by SHACL).
Links a catalog-record offering to the AI model it registers. Functional: one offering registers exactly one model. Asymmetric: an offering is distinct from the model it offers.
IANA media type of the expected input payload (e.g. "application/json").
Name of the evaluation protocol (e.g. "holdout", "5-fold-cv", "bootstrap-1000").
The it6:Flow (pipeline / reproducibility artefact) referenced by the evaluation context. Functional. Not declared a subproperty of it6:hasFlow because the evaluation context is not itself a run.
Inverse of daimo:evidenceOf: from an activity, retrieve every audit-evidence record attesting it. Non-functional (multiple auditors can independently attest the same activity).
Links a derived artifact to the authorisation under which it was produced. Functional.
Links a deployment to the MLDCAT-AP computer infrastructure running it. Functional: one deployment runs on one canonical infrastructure instance (redundancy is modelled as multiple deployments).
Links a derived artifact to the run that produced it. Functional: each derived artifact has exactly one originating run. Asymmetric: an artifact and its generating run are distinct.
Inverse of daimo:authorizesRun. Functional: each run is authorized by exactly one agreement.
Expiry timestamp of the execution authorisation.
